articles.headline: India Unveils 'Green Transition Fund' to Accelerate Climate Goals and Sustainable Development
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the establishment of the 'India Green Transition Fund' (IGTF) with an initial corpus of ₹50,000 crores.
• The fund aims to mobilize private and public investments into green technologies, renewable energy projects, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Focus areas include energy efficiency, sustainable mobility, waste-to-wealth initiatives, and green hydrogen production.
• This move aligns with India's ambitious Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement and its long-term net-zero targets.
• The IGTF will operate as a blended finance mechanism, offering concessional capital, risk guarantees, and technical assistance to viable green projects.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Environmental impact assessment
Mobilization of resources
Infrastructure: Energy
prelimsPoints: India Green Transition Fund (IGTF) initial corpus: ₹50,000 crores.
Fund aims to mobilize private and public investments in green technologies.
Focus areas include energy efficiency, sustainable mobility, green hydrogen.
Mechanism: Blended finance.
Aligned with India's NDCs and net-zero targets.
mainsPoints: Significance of IGTF in achieving India's climate commitments and fostering green growth.
Role of blended finance in de-risking green investments and attracting private capital.
Potential impact on job creation, technological innovation, and energy security.
Challenges in implementation, including inter-ministerial coordination and capacity building.
Comparison with similar international climate finance mechanisms and best practices.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting renewable energy sources and sustainable infrastructure, symbolizing the 'Green Transition Fund'.
articles.trivia: India is the world's third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases, but also one of the fastest-growing major economies investing in renewable energy.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Issues Comprehensive Guidelines on 'Model Code of Conduct' Enforcement for Digital Media
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment outlining guidelines for the Election Commission of India (ECI) to enforce the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) on digital and social media platforms.
• The ruling emphasizes holding platform intermediaries accountable for content dissemination during election periods, particularly concerning misinformation and hate speech.
• It mandates faster response times for content removal requests from the ECI and calls for greater transparency in political advertising on digital platforms.
• The Court highlighted the need to balance freedom of speech with the integrity of the electoral process.
• This judgment comes amidst increasing concerns over the impact of digital campaigns on voter behavior and democratic discourse.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court issues guidelines on MCC enforcement for digital media.
Ruling impacts Election Commission of India (ECI) and digital platforms.
Focus on misinformation, hate speech, and political advertising transparency.
Balances freedom of speech (Article 19) with electoral integrity.
Calls for faster content removal response times from platforms.
mainsPoints: Analysis of the constitutional basis for regulating digital content during elections (Article 19 vs. electoral fairness).
Challenges in implementing these guidelines, including technological capabilities of platforms and ECI's enforcement powers.
Impact on freedom of expression and potential for censorship concerns.
Role of technology in shaping democratic processes and the need for adaptive regulatory frameworks.
Recommendations for further electoral reforms to address digital age challenges.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a digital tablet, symbolizing legal oversight on digital platforms.
articles.trivia: The Model Code of Conduct is a set of guidelines issued by the Election Commission of India for political parties and candidates to ensure free and fair elections.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue was held in New Delhi, co-chaired by the foreign ministers of both nations.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, reaffirming commitment to a free, open, and rules-based order.
• Key areas of collaboration included defense and security, resilient supply chains, critical and emerging technologies, and infrastructure development.
• Both sides expressed concerns over regional stability and pledged to work closely on counter-terrorism and maritime security.
• The dialogue also reviewed progress on ongoing bilateral projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Co-chaired by foreign ministers.
Focus on Indo-Pacific, defense, supply chains, emerging tech.
Reaffirmed commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific.
Reviewed Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project.
mainsPoints: Significance of India-Japan partnership for regional stability and economic prosperity in the Indo-Pacific.
Challenges and opportunities in deepening defense and security cooperation, including joint exercises and technology transfer.
Role of India-Japan collaboration in building resilient supply chains and reducing dependence on single sources.
Impact of the dialogue on multilateral forums like the Quad and G20.
Future prospects for collaboration in emerging technologies and green initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure, notably contributing to the Delhi Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC) and the bullet train project.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation and Growth Trajectory
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive policy meeting.
• The decision reflects a balanced approach, considering persistent inflationary pressures, especially in food items, alongside robust domestic economic growth.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth for FY26 at 7.0% and retail inflation (CPI) at 4.8% for the same period, with risks evenly balanced.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Experts suggest the RBI is closely monitoring global economic uncertainties and domestic liquidity conditions before considering any rate adjustments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC maintains repo rate at 6.5%.
Repo rate unchanged for sixth consecutive meeting.
Projected FY26 GDP growth: 7.0%.
Projected FY26 Retail Inflation (CPI): 4.8%.
Monetary policy stance: 'Withdrawal of accommodation'.
mainsPoints: Analysis of the factors influencing RBI's decision to maintain the status quo, including domestic demand, global commodity prices, and financial stability.
Impact of the unchanged repo rate on borrowers, investors, and the overall economic landscape.
Challenges for RBI in managing inflation expectations while supporting growth in a volatile global environment.
Effectiveness of 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in achieving monetary policy objectives.
Interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in driving India's economic trajectory.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai, symbolizing economic policy.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test flight of its advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This critical test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, demonstrating autonomous navigation, guidance, and control.
• The RLV program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable, similar to aircraft.
• This achievement marks a major step towards India's future space exploration missions, including potential manned missions and satellite deployment.
• Further tests are planned to refine the technology for operational use, paving the way for a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
Test involved controlled landing experiment.
Aims to reduce space launch costs through reusability.
Major step for future manned missions and satellite deployment.
Paves way for fully reusable Two-Stage-to-Orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle.
mainsPoints: Significance of RLV technology for India's space program, including economic benefits and strategic advantages.
Comparison with international RLV programs (e.g., SpaceX's Falcon 9, NASA's Space Shuttle legacy).
Challenges in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems, including material science and thermal management.
Potential impact on India's role in the global space market and access to space.
Future roadmap for ISRO's RLV development and its integration with other ongoing space initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator returning to Earth.
articles.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD program is part of its efforts to develop a fully reusable launch vehicle to make space access more affordable and sustainable.

articles.headline: Enhancing Border Surveillance: India Deploys Advanced Indigenous Drones Along Western Frontier
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Army has commenced the deployment of a new fleet of indigenously developed advanced surveillance drones along the western border.
• These drones are equipped with high-resolution thermal imaging cameras, artificial intelligence-driven analytics, and extended flight capabilities for real-time monitoring.
• The move aims to bolster border security, detect illegal infiltration attempts, and provide crucial intelligence for counter-insurgency operations.
• This initiative is a part of India's broader strategy to modernize its defense forces through 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) in defense manufacturing.
• Training programs for personnel to operate and maintain these sophisticated unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are also underway.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- indigenization of technology
prelimsPoints: Indian Army deploys indigenous advanced surveillance drones along western border.
Drones feature thermal imaging, AI analytics, extended flight.
Aims to bolster border security and counter-infiltration.
Part of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in defense.
Personnel training for UAV operations underway.
mainsPoints: Effectiveness of drone technology in modern border management and surveillance.
Contribution of indigenous defense manufacturing to national security and strategic autonomy.
Challenges associated with drone deployment, including jamming, cyber threats, and ethical considerations.
Impact on cross-border terrorism and illegal activities.
Recommendations for integrating drone technology with other border security measures for a comprehensive defense strategy.
articles.imageDescription: A sleek, modern surveillance drone flying over a desert landscape near a border fence.
articles.trivia: India is among the top five global military spenders and is increasingly focusing on indigenous defense production to reduce reliance on imports.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Challenges in 'Project Cheetah' Reintroduction, Calls for Adaptive Management
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by an independent expert committee has highlighted several challenges facing 'Project Cheetah', India's ambitious initiative to reintroduce cheetahs into the wild.
• The report points to issues such as high mortality rates among relocated cheetahs, habitat suitability concerns, and human-wildlife conflict potential.
• It recommends a more adaptive management approach, including enhanced monitoring, genetic diversity management, and community engagement programs.
• 'Project Cheetah' aims to establish a viable population of cheetahs in India, after their extinction in the country in 1952.
• The government has acknowledged the findings and reiterated its commitment to the long-term success of the project, emphasizing continuous scientific evaluation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New report on 'Project Cheetah' highlights challenges.
Issues include high mortality, habitat suitability, human-wildlife conflict.
Recommends adaptive management, monitoring, genetic diversity.
Cheetahs extinct in India since 1952.
Kuno National Park is a key reintroduction site.
mainsPoints: Critical analysis of the scientific and logistical challenges in reintroduction programs for large carnivores.
Importance of habitat assessment and prey base availability for the long-term success of such projects.
Role of community participation and mitigation of human-wildlife conflict in conservation efforts.
Lessons learned from 'Project Cheetah' for future wildlife conservation initiatives in India.
Ethical considerations and ecological impacts of species reintroduction.
articles.imageDescription: A cheetah walking through tall grass in a savanna-like environment.
articles.trivia: Cheetahs are the world's fastest land animals, capable of running at speeds of up to 120 km/h over short distances.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Launches 'Vidya Setu' Program to Bridge Digital Divide in Rural Education
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education launched 'Vidya Setu', a new nationwide program aimed at bridging the digital divide in rural and remote educational institutions.
• The initiative focuses on providing digital infrastructure, internet connectivity, and access to e-learning resources for students and teachers in underserved areas.
• It includes distribution of tablets, development of localized digital content, and training for educators in digital pedagogy.
• 'Vidya Setu' leverages public-private partnerships and community involvement to ensure sustainable implementation.
• The program aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's emphasis on equitable access to quality education through technology.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education launched 'Vidya Setu' program.
Aims to bridge digital divide in rural education.
Provides digital infrastructure, internet, e-learning resources.
Includes tablet distribution, localized content, teacher training.
Aligns with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
mainsPoints: Assessment of the significance of 'Vidya Setu' in achieving equitable and inclusive education in India.
Challenges in bridging the digital divide in diverse geographical and socio-economic contexts.
Role of technology in transforming educational outcomes and promoting lifelong learning.
Importance of public-private partnerships and community engagement for sustainable program implementation.
Measures to ensure quality control of digital content and effective teacher training under the program.
articles.imageDescription: Children in a rural setting using tablets for learning, connected by digital lines.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest school-going population, highlighting the critical need for robust educational infrastructure and digital access.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers New Insights into a Harappan Site in Haryana
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced significant new findings from ongoing excavations at a prominent Harappan civilization site in Rakhigarhi, Haryana.
• Recent discoveries include well-preserved pottery, intricate beadwork, and structural remains that shed new light on the urban planning and craftsmanship of the Mature Harappan period.
• Researchers have also found evidence suggesting advanced water management systems and extensive trade networks, indicating a thriving ancient civilization.
• The findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the socio-economic and cultural practices of the Harappans.
• Rakhigarhi is one of the largest Harappan sites and continuous excavations have yielded crucial information about the Indus Valley Civilization.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: ASI findings at Rakhigarhi, Haryana.
Site belongs to Harappan Civilization (Indus Valley Civilization).
Discoveries: pottery, beadwork, structural remains, water management.
Rakhigarhi is one of the largest Harappan sites.
Insights into urban planning, craftsmanship, trade networks.
mainsPoints: Significance of Rakhigarhi in understanding the spread and characteristics of the Harappan Civilization.
Contribution of archaeological findings to reconstructing ancient Indian history and cultural practices.
Debate around the decline of the Indus Valley Civilization in light of new evidence.
Importance of preserving archaeological sites and promoting heritage tourism.
Methods used in archaeological excavations and interpretation of artifacts.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists working at an excavation site, uncovering ancient pottery and structures.
articles.trivia: Rakhigarhi is believed to be older than Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa, making it a critical site for understanding the origins and development of the Indus Valley Civilization.

articles.headline: Parliament Debates Proposed Amendments to Juvenile Justice Act, Focusing on Rehabilitation
articles.explainer:
• The Parliament initiated discussions on proposed amendments to the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015.
• The amendments aim to strengthen the rehabilitation and social reintegration mechanisms for children in conflict with the law and children in need of care and protection.
• Key proposals include enhancing the role of foster care, streamlining adoption procedures, and introducing stricter provisions for child-related offenses by adults.
• There is also a focus on ensuring child-friendly legal processes and specialized training for judicial and law enforcement personnel.
• The debate highlights the ongoing efforts to balance punitive measures with the overarching principle of 'best interest of the child'.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
keyTerms: Children in Need of Care and Protection
prelimsPoints: Parliament debating amendments to Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015.
Focus on rehabilitation and social reintegration of children.
Proposals include enhanced foster care, streamlined adoption, stricter adult penalties.
Emphasizes child-friendly legal processes and specialized training.
Aims to balance punitive measures with 'best interest of the child'.
mainsPoints: Critical evaluation of the existing Juvenile Justice Act and the necessity for proposed amendments.
Analysis of the 'best interest of the child' principle in the context of juvenile justice and its practical application.
Challenges in implementing rehabilitation programs and ensuring effective social reintegration of juvenile offenders.
Role of foster care and adoption in ensuring the welfare of children in need of care and protection.
Recommendations for strengthening the institutional framework and capacity building for child protection services.
articles.imageDescription: A silhouette of a child standing before a blurred image of the Parliament building, symbolizing legislative impact on child welfare.
articles.trivia: The Juvenile Justice Act in India has undergone several amendments since its inception, reflecting evolving societal views on child offenders and child welfare.

articles.headline: India's Services Sector Growth Remains Robust in October, PMI Data Indicates
articles.explainer:
• India's services sector continued its strong growth trajectory in October, with the S&P Global India Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) remaining robust at 60.5.
• This marks a sustained period of expansion, driven by strong demand conditions, new business gains, and improved business confidence.
• The growth was broad-based across various sub-sectors, including finance, business services, and transportation.
• Employment in the services sector also saw an uptick, indicating positive labor market trends.
• While input cost inflation remained a concern, businesses managed to pass on some costs, leading to a slight increase in output prices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Growth and development
prelimsPoints: India's Services PMI at 60.5 in October.
Indicates robust growth in services sector.
Driven by strong demand, new business, improved confidence.
Broad-based growth across sub-sectors.
Employment in services sector also increased.
mainsPoints: Analysis of the factors contributing to the sustained growth in India's services sector.
Significance of the services sector as a driver of economic growth and employment generation in India.
Impact of input cost inflation on the profitability and pricing strategies of service businesses.
Comparison of services sector performance with the manufacturing sector and its implications for overall economic stability.
Government policies and reforms needed to further boost the competitiveness and resilience of the services sector.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing an upward trend, representing robust economic growth, with 'Services PMI' text.
articles.trivia: The services sector contributes over 50% to India's GDP, making it the largest sector of the Indian economy.

articles.headline: UN General Assembly Discusses Global Food Security Challenges, India Reiterates Commitment
articles.explainer:
• The United Nations General Assembly convened a high-level session to address escalating global food security challenges, exacerbated by climate change and geopolitical conflicts.
• Member states discussed strategies to enhance agricultural productivity, build resilient food systems, and ensure equitable access to nutritious food.
• India's representative reiterated the nation's commitment to global food security, highlighting its role as a major food producer and its initiatives like the National Food Security Act.
• Emphasis was placed on sustainable agricultural practices, reducing food waste, and international cooperation to prevent future food crises.
• The session aimed to galvanize collective action towards achieving Sustainable Development Goal 2: Zero Hunger.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
prelimsPoints: UN General Assembly session on global food security.
India reiterated commitment, highlighted NFSA.
Discussions on agricultural productivity, resilient food systems, equitable access.
Focus on sustainable practices, reducing food waste, international cooperation.
Aims to achieve SDG 2: Zero Hunger.
mainsPoints: Analysis of the multi-faceted challenges to global food security, including climate change, conflicts, and supply chain disruptions.
India's contribution to global food security, both through domestic policies and international cooperation.
Effectiveness of the National Food Security Act (NFSA) in addressing hunger and malnutrition within India.
Measures required to build resilient food systems that can withstand future shocks and ensure sustainable food production.
Role of international organizations and multilateral initiatives in fostering global food security.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of diverse food items from around the world, with the UN logo in the background.
articles.trivia: The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) estimates that over 700 million people worldwide face chronic hunger.

articles.headline: At Least Two Killed, Three Injured in Mumbai Local Train Accident
articles.explainer:
• A tragic incident occurred in Mumbai where a local train reportedly hit at least five individuals, resulting in two fatalities and three injuries.
• The accident took place near a busy railway crossing during peak hours, raising concerns about railway safety and pedestrian awareness.
• Emergency services quickly responded, transporting the injured to nearby hospitals for immediate medical attention.
• Railway authorities have initiated an inquiry to ascertain the exact cause of the accident and review safety protocols.
• This incident highlights the persistent challenges of railway safety in densely populated urban areas and the need for stricter enforcement of safety regulations.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
mainsPoints: Analysis of the causes of railway accidents in urban areas, including trespassing, infrastructure limitations, and human error.
Measures to enhance railway safety, such as improved fencing, overpasses/underpasses, and public awareness campaigns.
Role of railway authorities and local administration in enforcing safety regulations and preventing accidents.
Impact of such incidents on public perception of railway travel and urban mobility.
Technological solutions for accident prevention and rapid response in railway networks.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency personnel attending to an accident scene near railway tracks.
articles.trivia: Mumbai's local train network, often called the 'lifeline of Mumbai', carries over 7.5 million commuters daily, making it one of the busiest suburban rail systems in the world.

articles.headline: Veteran Actress and Playback Singer Sulakshana Pandit Passes Away at 71
articles.explainer:
• Renowned veteran actress and playback singer Sulakshana Pandit passed away at the age of 71.
• She was known for her notable contributions to Hindi cinema in the 1970s and 80s, starring in films like 'Sankalp', 'Sankoch', and 'Waqt Ki Deewar'.
• Beyond acting, Pandit was also a talented playback singer, lending her voice to many popular songs and establishing herself as a versatile artist.
• Her career spanned several decades, leaving a significant mark on the Indian film and music industry.
• The film fraternity and fans have expressed their condolences, remembering her distinctive voice and powerful performances.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Sulakshana Pandit from her acting days.
articles.trivia: Sulakshana Pandit belonged to a musical family; her elder sister is the famous playback singer Vijeta Pandit, and her brother is music director Jatin Pandit of the Jatin-Lalit duo.

articles.headline: New Indigenous Vaccine Candidate for Dengue Shows Promising Results in Clinical Trials
articles.explainer:
• Indian researchers announced promising results from Phase II clinical trials of a new indigenous vaccine candidate for Dengue fever.
• The vaccine, developed by a public-private partnership, demonstrated high efficacy and a favorable safety profile across diverse age groups.
• Dengue is a major public health concern in India, with thousands of cases reported annually, especially during monsoon seasons.
• This breakthrough could significantly reduce the disease burden and public health costs associated with Dengue management.
• Phase III trials are expected to commence soon, aiming for a rapid regulatory approval process to make the vaccine available to the public.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Bio-technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: Indigenous Dengue vaccine candidate shows promising Phase II clinical trial results.
Developed through public-private partnership.
Demonstrated high efficacy and favorable safety profile.
Dengue is major public health concern in India.
Phase III trials expected soon for regulatory approval.
mainsPoints: Significance of an indigenous Dengue vaccine for India's public health system and disease control efforts.
Challenges in vaccine development for vector-borne diseases like Dengue, considering multiple serotypes.
Role of public-private partnerships in accelerating biomedical research and development.
Potential impact of widespread vaccination on disease burden, healthcare costs, and economic productivity.
Ethical considerations and regulatory pathways for rapid vaccine deployment in a public health emergency.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat examining vaccine vials, with a microscopic image of the Dengue virus in the background.
articles.trivia: Dengue fever is caused by one of four related viruses (DENV-1, DENV-2, DENV-3, and DENV-4) and is primarily transmitted by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Meet to Address Global Economic Stability Amidst Geopolitical Tensions
articles.explainer:
• Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors from G20 nations convened to discuss strategies for maintaining global economic stability.
• The meeting focused on mitigating the impact of ongoing geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions, and persistent inflationary pressures.
• Key discussions included coordinated fiscal and monetary policies, debt sustainability for developing nations, and strengthening multilateral financial institutions.
• India, as a prominent G20 member, advocated for inclusive growth, climate finance, and digital public infrastructure.
• The ministers pledged to enhance international cooperation to address shared economic challenges and promote sustainable global recovery.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meet.
Discussed global economic stability, geopolitical tensions, inflation.
Topics: fiscal/monetary policies, debt sustainability, multilateral institutions.
India advocated for inclusive growth, climate finance, digital public infrastructure.
Pledge for enhanced international cooperation.
mainsPoints: Role of G20 in global economic governance and crisis management.
Impact of geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions on global economic stability and trade.
India's priorities and contributions to G20 discussions, particularly on climate finance and digital public infrastructure.
Challenges faced by developing nations regarding debt sustainability and access to finance.
Effectiveness of coordinated international policies in addressing complex global economic challenges.
articles.imageDescription: A conference table with flags of G20 nations, symbolizing international economic cooperation.
articles.trivia: The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing about two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Tele-Mental Health Helpline to Expand Access to Psychological Support
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched a new National Tele-Mental Health Helpline, aiming to provide accessible psychological support across the country.
• The 24/7 helpline offers counseling, psychological first aid, and referrals to specialized mental health services through trained professionals.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards destigmatizing mental health issues and ensuring timely intervention, especially in remote areas.
• It leverages digital technology to overcome geographical barriers and enhance the reach of mental healthcare services.
• The launch aligns with India's commitment to strengthening its public health infrastructure and addressing the growing burden of mental health conditions.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Health launched National Tele-Mental Health Helpline.
Provides 24/7 counseling, psychological first aid, referrals.
Aims to destigmatize mental health and provide timely intervention.
Leverages digital technology for broader reach.
Part of strengthening public health infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Significance of tele-mental health services in addressing the mental health crisis in India, particularly in underserved regions.
Challenges in delivering quality mental healthcare services, including shortage of professionals, infrastructure gaps, and social stigma.
Role of technology in expanding access to healthcare and its ethical implications.
Government strategies to destigmatize mental illness and promote mental well-being across different demographic groups.
Recommendations for integrating mental health services into primary healthcare and community-based programs.
articles.imageDescription: A smartphone displaying a mental health app with a helpline number, symbolizing digital access to support.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) estimates that one in seven Indians is affected by mental health disorders.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Maritime Security Dialogue Reaffirms Cooperation in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka held their annual Maritime Security Dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to enhanced cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.
• Discussions focused on joint surveillance, information sharing, capacity building, and combating maritime challenges like piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of maintaining a free, open, and secure maritime domain for regional stability and economic prosperity.
• The dialogue also touched upon deepening naval exercises and port visits to strengthen interoperability.
• This engagement is crucial for India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its vision for Security And Growth for All in the Region (SAGAR).
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas - linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: India-Sri Lanka Maritime Security Dialogue held.
Focus on cooperation in Indian Ocean Region.
Discussions on joint surveillance, information sharing, capacity building.
Combating piracy, illegal fishing, drug trafficking.
Aligns with India's 'Neighbourhood First' and SAGAR policy.
mainsPoints: Significance of India-Sri Lanka maritime cooperation for regional security and stability in the Indian Ocean.
Challenges posed by non-traditional maritime threats and strategies to counter them.
Importance of bilateral defense engagements and capacity building initiatives.
Analysis of India's 'Neighbourhood First' and SAGAR policies in fostering regional partnerships.
Impact of external powers' presence in the Indian Ocean on India's strategic interests.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships of India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise at sea.
articles.trivia: India and Sri Lanka share a maritime boundary and have historically strong cultural and economic ties.

highlights: India launches 'Green Transition Fund' (IGTF) with ₹50,000 crores to accelerate climate goals and sustainable development.
Supreme Court issues landmark guidelines for Election Commission of India on enforcing Model Code of Conduct on digital media platforms.
India and Japan conclude strategic dialogue, emphasizing Indo-Pacific security, resilient supply chains, and critical technologies cooperation.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains repo rate at 6.5%, signals cautious optimism on inflation and robust economic growth.
ISRO successfully tests a next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator, a major step for future space missions.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'India Green Transition Fund' (IGTF), consider the following statements:
1. It has been approved with an initial corpus of ₹50,000 crores.
2. It aims to mobilize only private investments into green technologies.
3. It will operate as a blended finance mechanism.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 only
(b) 1 and 3 only
(c) 2 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The IGTF has an initial corpus of ₹50,000 crores. Statement 2 is incorrect: The fund aims to mobilize both private and public investments. Statement 3 is correct: The IGTF will operate as a blended finance mechanism.

prelimsMocks.question:
The recent Supreme Court guidelines on Model Code of Conduct (MCC) enforcement for digital media primarily aim to address which of the following during election periods?
(a) Regulation of traditional print media content only.
(b) Accountability of platform intermediaries for misinformation and hate speech.
(c) Mandating political parties to disclose their funding sources.
(d) Restricting live telecasts of election rallies.
options: (a) Regulation of traditional print media content only.
(b) Accountability of platform intermediaries for misinformation and hate speech.
(c) Mandating political parties to disclose their funding sources.
(d) Restricting live telecasts of election rallies.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Accountability of platform intermediaries for misinformation and hate speech.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's ruling specifically emphasizes holding digital platform intermediaries accountable for content dissemination, particularly concerning misinformation and hate speech, during election periods. It focuses on digital media, not traditional print media, and while funding and rallies are related to elections, they are not the primary focus of these specific guidelines.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements correctly describes India's 'Project Cheetah'?
(a) It is an initiative to reintroduce snow leopards in the Himalayan region.
(b) It aims to establish a viable population of cheetahs in India, after their extinction in the country.
(c) It focuses on the conservation of Asiatic lions in Gir National Park.
(d) It is a program to protect migratory birds in Indian wetlands.
options: (a) It is an initiative to reintroduce snow leopards in the Himalayan region.
(b) It aims to establish a viable population of cheetahs in India, after their extinction in the country.
(c) It focuses on the conservation of Asiatic lions in Gir National Park.
(d) It is a program to protect migratory birds in Indian wetlands.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It aims to establish a viable population of cheetahs in India, after their extinction in the country.
prelimsMocks.explanation: 'Project Cheetah' is India's ambitious initiative to reintroduce cheetahs into the wild, after their extinction in India in 1952. Kuno National Park is a key reintroduction site. Options (a), (c), and (d) refer to different conservation efforts.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) of ISRO:
1. It aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
2. It demonstrates autonomous navigation, guidance, and control for spacecraft return.
3. It has already achieved a fully operational two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) capability.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 1 and 2 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The RLV program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable. Statement 2 is correct: The recent test involved a controlled landing experiment demonstrating autonomous navigation, guidance, and control. Statement 3 is incorrect: The successful test is a major step towards a fully reusable TSTO launch vehicle, but it has not yet achieved this operational capability; further tests are planned.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Vidya Setu' program, recently launched by the Ministry of Education, primarily aims to:
(a) Promote traditional Indian art forms in schools.
(b) Bridge the digital divide in rural and remote educational institutions.
(c) Provide scholarships for higher education to meritorious students.
(d) Establish new universities in underserved regions.
options: (a) Promote traditional Indian art forms in schools.
(b) Bridge the digital divide in rural and remote educational institutions.
(c) Provide scholarships for higher education to meritorious students.
(d) Establish new universities in underserved regions.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Bridge the digital divide in rural and remote educational institutions.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Vidya Setu' program is a nationwide initiative focused on providing digital infrastructure, internet connectivity, and access to e-learning resources for students and teachers in underserved rural and remote areas, thereby bridging the digital divide.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the significance of the 'India Green Transition Fund' (IGTF) in achieving India's climate commitments and fostering sustainable development. Discuss the challenges in its effective implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Environmental pollution and degradation, Conservation; Mobilization of resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce IGTF and its objectives. Discuss its significance in terms of climate finance, private investment mobilization, and achieving NDCs. Detail its potential impact on green technologies and sustainable growth. Subsequently, elaborate on the challenges such as inter-ministerial coordination, capacity building, technology transfer, and ensuring equitable distribution of benefits. Conclude with measures for effective implementation.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's guidelines on enforcing the Model Code of Conduct on digital media reflect a crucial step towards ensuring fair electoral practices in the digital age. Examine the implications of this ruling on freedom of speech and the integrity of the electoral process.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—significant provisions; Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the context and key aspects of the Supreme Court's guidelines. Discuss how they aim to enhance electoral integrity by curbing misinformation and hate speech on digital platforms. Then, critically analyze the potential implications for freedom of speech (Article 19), examining concerns about censorship and platform liability. Conclude by suggesting a balanced approach to uphold both electoral fairness and fundamental rights in the digital sphere.

mainsMocks.question: India's services sector continues to be a robust driver of economic growth and employment. Discuss the factors contributing to its sustained performance and assess the challenges it faces in contributing to inclusive growth.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the strong performance of India's services sector, citing recent PMI data. Elaborate on factors driving this growth, such as strong domestic demand, digitalization, and policy support. Subsequently, discuss the challenges including skill gaps, informalization, regional disparities in growth, and vulnerability to global economic shocks. Conclude with policy recommendations to ensure the services sector contributes more inclusively to India's overall development.

quickBytes.text: The 'India Green Transition Fund' (IGTF) has an initial corpus of ₹50,000 crores to boost climate goals.
Supreme Court's new MCC guidelines address misinformation and hate speech on digital media during elections.
ISRO's RLV-TD aims to significantly reduce space launch costs by developing reusable rocket technology.
Rakhigarhi, a major Harappan site in Haryana, continues to yield new archaeological insights into the IVC.
The 'Vidya Setu' program by the Ministry of Education targets bridging the digital divide in rural education.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains the repo rate at 6.5% amidst inflation and growth concerns.
'Project Cheetah' faces challenges like high mortality rates and habitat suitability issues, requiring adaptive management.
India and Japan reaffirmed commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific order in their strategic dialogue.
The National Tele-Mental Health Helpline offers 24/7 psychological support to expand healthcare access.
Dengue fever is primarily transmitted by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes, prevalent in tropical and subtropical regions.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers New Insights into a Harappan Site in Haryana
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced significant new findings from ongoing excavations at a prominent Harappan civilization site in Rakhigarhi, Haryana.
• Recent discoveries include well-preserved pottery, intricate beadwork, and structural remains that shed new light on the urban planning and craftsmanship of the Mature Harappan period.
• Researchers have also found evidence suggesting advanced water management systems and extensive trade networks, indicating a thriving ancient civilization.
• The findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the socio-economic and cultural practices of the Harappans.
• Rakhigarhi is one of the largest Harappan sites and continuous excavations have yielded crucial information about the Indus Valley Civilization.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: ASI findings at Rakhigarhi, Haryana.
Site belongs to Harappan Civilization (Indus Valley Civilization).
Discoveries: pottery, beadwork, structural remains, water management.
Rakhigarhi is one of the largest Harappan sites.
Insights into urban planning, craftsmanship, trade networks.
mainsPoints: Significance of Rakhigarhi in understanding the spread and characteristics of the Harappan Civilization.
Contribution of archaeological findings to reconstructing ancient Indian history and cultural practices.
Debate around the decline of the Indus Valley Civilization in light of new evidence.
Importance of preserving archaeological sites and promoting heritage tourism.
Methods used in archaeological excavations and interpretation of artifacts.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists working at an excavation site, uncovering ancient pottery and structures.
GS1.trivia: Rakhigarhi is believed to be older than Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa, making it a critical site for understanding the origins and development of the Indus Valley Civilization.

GS1.headline: Veteran Actress and Playback Singer Sulakshana Pandit Passes Away at 71
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned veteran actress and playback singer Sulakshana Pandit passed away at the age of 71.
• She was known for her notable contributions to Hindi cinema in the 1970s and 80s, starring in films like 'Sankalp', 'Sankoch', and 'Waqt Ki Deewar'.
• Beyond acting, Pandit was also a talented playback singer, lending her voice to many popular songs and establishing herself as a versatile artist.
• Her career spanned several decades, leaving a significant mark on the Indian film and music industry.
• The film fraternity and fans have expressed their condolences, remembering her distinctive voice and powerful performances.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
GS1.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Sulakshana Pandit from her acting days.
GS1.trivia: Sulakshana Pandit belonged to a musical family; her elder sister is the famous playback singer Vijeta Pandit, and her brother is music director Jatin Pandit of the Jatin-Lalit duo.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Issues Comprehensive Guidelines on 'Model Code of Conduct' Enforcement for Digital Media
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment outlining guidelines for the Election Commission of India (ECI) to enforce the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) on digital and social media platforms.
• The ruling emphasizes holding platform intermediaries accountable for content dissemination during election periods, particularly concerning misinformation and hate speech.
• It mandates faster response times for content removal requests from the ECI and calls for greater transparency in political advertising on digital platforms.
• The Court highlighted the need to balance freedom of speech with the integrity of the electoral process.
• This judgment comes amidst increasing concerns over the impact of digital campaigns on voter behavior and democratic discourse.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court issues guidelines on MCC enforcement for digital media.
Ruling impacts Election Commission of India (ECI) and digital platforms.
Focus on misinformation, hate speech, and political advertising transparency.
Balances freedom of speech (Article 19) with electoral integrity.
Calls for faster content removal response times from platforms.
mainsPoints: Analysis of the constitutional basis for regulating digital content during elections (Article 19 vs. electoral fairness).
Challenges in implementing these guidelines, including technological capabilities of platforms and ECI's enforcement powers.
Impact on freedom of expression and potential for censorship concerns.
Role of technology in shaping democratic processes and the need for adaptive regulatory frameworks.
Recommendations for further electoral reforms to address digital age challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel striking a digital tablet, symbolizing legal oversight on digital platforms.
GS2.trivia: The Model Code of Conduct is a set of guidelines issued by the Election Commission of India for political parties and candidates to ensure free and fair elections.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue was held in New Delhi, co-chaired by the foreign ministers of both nations.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, reaffirming commitment to a free, open, and rules-based order.
• Key areas of collaboration included defense and security, resilient supply chains, critical and emerging technologies, and infrastructure development.
• Both sides expressed concerns over regional stability and pledged to work closely on counter-terrorism and maritime security.
• The dialogue also reviewed progress on ongoing bilateral projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Co-chaired by foreign ministers.
Focus on Indo-Pacific, defense, supply chains, emerging tech.
Reaffirmed commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific.
Reviewed Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project.
mainsPoints: Significance of India-Japan partnership for regional stability and economic prosperity in the Indo-Pacific.
Challenges and opportunities in deepening defense and security cooperation, including joint exercises and technology transfer.
Role of India-Japan collaboration in building resilient supply chains and reducing dependence on single sources.
Impact of the dialogue on multilateral forums like the Quad and G20.
Future prospects for collaboration in emerging technologies and green initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure, notably contributing to the Delhi Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC) and the bullet train project.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Launches 'Vidya Setu' Program to Bridge Digital Divide in Rural Education
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education launched 'Vidya Setu', a new nationwide program aimed at bridging the digital divide in rural and remote educational institutions.
• The initiative focuses on providing digital infrastructure, internet connectivity, and access to e-learning resources for students and teachers in underserved areas.
• It includes distribution of tablets, development of localized digital content, and training for educators in digital pedagogy.
• 'Vidya Setu' leverages public-private partnerships and community involvement to ensure sustainable implementation.
• The program aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's emphasis on equitable access to quality education through technology.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education launched 'Vidya Setu' program.
Aims to bridge digital divide in rural education.
Provides digital infrastructure, internet, e-learning resources.
Includes tablet distribution, localized content, teacher training.
Aligns with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
mainsPoints: Assessment of the significance of 'Vidya Setu' in achieving equitable and inclusive education in India.
Challenges in bridging the digital divide in diverse geographical and socio-economic contexts.
Role of technology in transforming educational outcomes and promoting lifelong learning.
Importance of public-private partnerships and community engagement for sustainable program implementation.
Measures to ensure quality control of digital content and effective teacher training under the program.
GS2.imageDescription: Children in a rural setting using tablets for learning, connected by digital lines.
GS2.trivia: India has the world's largest school-going population, highlighting the critical need for robust educational infrastructure and digital access.

GS2.headline: Parliament Debates Proposed Amendments to Juvenile Justice Act, Focusing on Rehabilitation
GS2.explainer:
• The Parliament initiated discussions on proposed amendments to the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015.
• The amendments aim to strengthen the rehabilitation and social reintegration mechanisms for children in conflict with the law and children in need of care and protection.
• Key proposals include enhancing the role of foster care, streamlining adoption procedures, and introducing stricter provisions for child-related offenses by adults.
• There is also a focus on ensuring child-friendly legal processes and specialized training for judicial and law enforcement personnel.
• The debate highlights the ongoing efforts to balance punitive measures with the overarching principle of 'best interest of the child'.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
keyTerms: Children in Need of Care and Protection
prelimsPoints: Parliament debating amendments to Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015.
Focus on rehabilitation and social reintegration of children.
Proposals include enhanced foster care, streamlined adoption, stricter adult penalties.
Emphasizes child-friendly legal processes and specialized training.
Aims to balance punitive measures with 'best interest of the child'.
mainsPoints: Critical evaluation of the existing Juvenile Justice Act and the necessity for proposed amendments.
Analysis of the 'best interest of the child' principle in the context of juvenile justice and its practical application.
Challenges in implementing rehabilitation programs and ensuring effective social reintegration of juvenile offenders.
Role of foster care and adoption in ensuring the welfare of children in need of care and protection.
Recommendations for strengthening the institutional framework and capacity building for child protection services.
GS2.imageDescription: A silhouette of a child standing before a blurred image of the Parliament building, symbolizing legislative impact on child welfare.
GS2.trivia: The Juvenile Justice Act in India has undergone several amendments since its inception, reflecting evolving societal views on child offenders and child welfare.

GS2.headline: UN General Assembly Discusses Global Food Security Challenges, India Reiterates Commitment
GS2.explainer:
• The United Nations General Assembly convened a high-level session to address escalating global food security challenges, exacerbated by climate change and geopolitical conflicts.
• Member states discussed strategies to enhance agricultural productivity, build resilient food systems, and ensure equitable access to nutritious food.
• India's representative reiterated the nation's commitment to global food security, highlighting its role as a major food producer and its initiatives like the National Food Security Act.
• Emphasis was placed on sustainable agricultural practices, reducing food waste, and international cooperation to prevent future food crises.
• The session aimed to galvanize collective action towards achieving Sustainable Development Goal 2: Zero Hunger.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
prelimsPoints: UN General Assembly session on global food security.
India reiterated commitment, highlighted NFSA.
Discussions on agricultural productivity, resilient food systems, equitable access.
Focus on sustainable practices, reducing food waste, international cooperation.
Aims to achieve SDG 2: Zero Hunger.
mainsPoints: Analysis of the multi-faceted challenges to global food security, including climate change, conflicts, and supply chain disruptions.
India's contribution to global food security, both through domestic policies and international cooperation.
Effectiveness of the National Food Security Act (NFSA) in addressing hunger and malnutrition within India.
Measures required to build resilient food systems that can withstand future shocks and ensure sustainable food production.
Role of international organizations and multilateral initiatives in fostering global food security.
GS2.imageDescription: A collage of diverse food items from around the world, with the UN logo in the background.
GS2.trivia: The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) estimates that over 700 million people worldwide face chronic hunger.

GS2.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Meet to Address Global Economic Stability Amidst Geopolitical Tensions
GS2.explainer:
• Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors from G20 nations convened to discuss strategies for maintaining global economic stability.
• The meeting focused on mitigating the impact of ongoing geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions, and persistent inflationary pressures.
• Key discussions included coordinated fiscal and monetary policies, debt sustainability for developing nations, and strengthening multilateral financial institutions.
• India, as a prominent G20 member, advocated for inclusive growth, climate finance, and digital public infrastructure.
• The ministers pledged to enhance international cooperation to address shared economic challenges and promote sustainable global recovery.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meet.
Discussed global economic stability, geopolitical tensions, inflation.
Topics: fiscal/monetary policies, debt sustainability, multilateral institutions.
India advocated for inclusive growth, climate finance, digital public infrastructure.
Pledge for enhanced international cooperation.
mainsPoints: Role of G20 in global economic governance and crisis management.
Impact of geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions on global economic stability and trade.
India's priorities and contributions to G20 discussions, particularly on climate finance and digital public infrastructure.
Challenges faced by developing nations regarding debt sustainability and access to finance.
Effectiveness of coordinated international policies in addressing complex global economic challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: A conference table with flags of G20 nations, symbolizing international economic cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing about two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: Government Launches National Tele-Mental Health Helpline to Expand Access to Psychological Support
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched a new National Tele-Mental Health Helpline, aiming to provide accessible psychological support across the country.
• The 24/7 helpline offers counseling, psychological first aid, and referrals to specialized mental health services through trained professionals.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards destigmatizing mental health issues and ensuring timely intervention, especially in remote areas.
• It leverages digital technology to overcome geographical barriers and enhance the reach of mental healthcare services.
• The launch aligns with India's commitment to strengthening its public health infrastructure and addressing the growing burden of mental health conditions.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Health launched National Tele-Mental Health Helpline.
Provides 24/7 counseling, psychological first aid, referrals.
Aims to destigmatize mental health and provide timely intervention.
Leverages digital technology for broader reach.
Part of strengthening public health infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Significance of tele-mental health services in addressing the mental health crisis in India, particularly in underserved regions.
Challenges in delivering quality mental healthcare services, including shortage of professionals, infrastructure gaps, and social stigma.
Role of technology in expanding access to healthcare and its ethical implications.
Government strategies to destigmatize mental illness and promote mental well-being across different demographic groups.
Recommendations for integrating mental health services into primary healthcare and community-based programs.
GS2.imageDescription: A smartphone displaying a mental health app with a helpline number, symbolizing digital access to support.
GS2.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) estimates that one in seven Indians is affected by mental health disorders.

GS2.headline: India-Sri Lanka Maritime Security Dialogue Reaffirms Cooperation in Indian Ocean Region
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka held their annual Maritime Security Dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to enhanced cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.
• Discussions focused on joint surveillance, information sharing, capacity building, and combating maritime challenges like piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of maintaining a free, open, and secure maritime domain for regional stability and economic prosperity.
• The dialogue also touched upon deepening naval exercises and port visits to strengthen interoperability.
• This engagement is crucial for India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its vision for Security And Growth for All in the Region (SAGAR).
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas - linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: India-Sri Lanka Maritime Security Dialogue held.
Focus on cooperation in Indian Ocean Region.
Discussions on joint surveillance, information sharing, capacity building.
Combating piracy, illegal fishing, drug trafficking.
Aligns with India's 'Neighbourhood First' and SAGAR policy.
mainsPoints: Significance of India-Sri Lanka maritime cooperation for regional security and stability in the Indian Ocean.
Challenges posed by non-traditional maritime threats and strategies to counter them.
Importance of bilateral defense engagements and capacity building initiatives.
Analysis of India's 'Neighbourhood First' and SAGAR policies in fostering regional partnerships.
Impact of external powers' presence in the Indian Ocean on India's strategic interests.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships of India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise at sea.
GS2.trivia: India and Sri Lanka share a maritime boundary and have historically strong cultural and economic ties.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'Green Transition Fund' to Accelerate Climate Goals and Sustainable Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the establishment of the 'India Green Transition Fund' (IGTF) with an initial corpus of ₹50,000 crores.
• The fund aims to mobilize private and public investments into green technologies, renewable energy projects, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Focus areas include energy efficiency, sustainable mobility, waste-to-wealth initiatives, and green hydrogen production.
• This move aligns with India's ambitious Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement and its long-term net-zero targets.
• The IGTF will operate as a blended finance mechanism, offering concessional capital, risk guarantees, and technical assistance to viable green projects.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Environmental impact assessment
Mobilization of resources
Infrastructure: Energy
prelimsPoints: India Green Transition Fund (IGTF) initial corpus: ₹50,000 crores.
Fund aims to mobilize private and public investments in green technologies.
Focus areas include energy efficiency, sustainable mobility, green hydrogen.
Mechanism: Blended finance.
Aligned with India's NDCs and net-zero targets.
mainsPoints: Significance of IGTF in achieving India's climate commitments and fostering green growth.
Role of blended finance in de-risking green investments and attracting private capital.
Potential impact on job creation, technological innovation, and energy security.
Challenges in implementation, including inter-ministerial coordination and capacity building.
Comparison with similar international climate finance mechanisms and best practices.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting renewable energy sources and sustainable infrastructure, symbolizing the 'Green Transition Fund'.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases, but also one of the fastest-growing major economies investing in renewable energy.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation and Growth Trajectory
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive policy meeting.
• The decision reflects a balanced approach, considering persistent inflationary pressures, especially in food items, alongside robust domestic economic growth.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth for FY26 at 7.0% and retail inflation (CPI) at 4.8% for the same period, with risks evenly balanced.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Experts suggest the RBI is closely monitoring global economic uncertainties and domestic liquidity conditions before considering any rate adjustments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC maintains repo rate at 6.5%.
Repo rate unchanged for sixth consecutive meeting.
Projected FY26 GDP growth: 7.0%.
Projected FY26 Retail Inflation (CPI): 4.8%.
Monetary policy stance: 'Withdrawal of accommodation'.
mainsPoints: Analysis of the factors influencing RBI's decision to maintain the status quo, including domestic demand, global commodity prices, and financial stability.
Impact of the unchanged repo rate on borrowers, investors, and the overall economic landscape.
Challenges for RBI in managing inflation expectations while supporting growth in a volatile global environment.
Effectiveness of 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in achieving monetary policy objectives.
Interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in driving India's economic trajectory.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai, symbolizing economic policy.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test flight of its advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This critical test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, demonstrating autonomous navigation, guidance, and control.
• The RLV program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable, similar to aircraft.
• This achievement marks a major step towards India's future space exploration missions, including potential manned missions and satellite deployment.
• Further tests are planned to refine the technology for operational use, paving the way for a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
Test involved controlled landing experiment.
Aims to reduce space launch costs through reusability.
Major step for future manned missions and satellite deployment.
Paves way for fully reusable Two-Stage-to-Orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle.
mainsPoints: Significance of RLV technology for India's space program, including economic benefits and strategic advantages.
Comparison with international RLV programs (e.g., SpaceX's Falcon 9, NASA's Space Shuttle legacy).
Challenges in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems, including material science and thermal management.
Potential impact on India's role in the global space market and access to space.
Future roadmap for ISRO's RLV development and its integration with other ongoing space initiatives.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator returning to Earth.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD program is part of its efforts to develop a fully reusable launch vehicle to make space access more affordable and sustainable.

GS3.headline: Enhancing Border Surveillance: India Deploys Advanced Indigenous Drones Along Western Frontier
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Army has commenced the deployment of a new fleet of indigenously developed advanced surveillance drones along the western border.
• These drones are equipped with high-resolution thermal imaging cameras, artificial intelligence-driven analytics, and extended flight capabilities for real-time monitoring.
• The move aims to bolster border security, detect illegal infiltration attempts, and provide crucial intelligence for counter-insurgency operations.
• This initiative is a part of India's broader strategy to modernize its defense forces through 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) in defense manufacturing.
• Training programs for personnel to operate and maintain these sophisticated unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are also underway.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- indigenization of technology
prelimsPoints: Indian Army deploys indigenous advanced surveillance drones along western border.
Drones feature thermal imaging, AI analytics, extended flight.
Aims to bolster border security and counter-infiltration.
Part of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in defense.
Personnel training for UAV operations underway.
mainsPoints: Effectiveness of drone technology in modern border management and surveillance.
Contribution of indigenous defense manufacturing to national security and strategic autonomy.
Challenges associated with drone deployment, including jamming, cyber threats, and ethical considerations.
Impact on cross-border terrorism and illegal activities.
Recommendations for integrating drone technology with other border security measures for a comprehensive defense strategy.
GS3.imageDescription: A sleek, modern surveillance drone flying over a desert landscape near a border fence.
GS3.trivia: India is among the top five global military spenders and is increasingly focusing on indigenous defense production to reduce reliance on imports.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Challenges in 'Project Cheetah' Reintroduction, Calls for Adaptive Management
GS3.explainer:
• A recent report by an independent expert committee has highlighted several challenges facing 'Project Cheetah', India's ambitious initiative to reintroduce cheetahs into the wild.
• The report points to issues such as high mortality rates among relocated cheetahs, habitat suitability concerns, and human-wildlife conflict potential.
• It recommends a more adaptive management approach, including enhanced monitoring, genetic diversity management, and community engagement programs.
• 'Project Cheetah' aims to establish a viable population of cheetahs in India, after their extinction in the country in 1952.
• The government has acknowledged the findings and reiterated its commitment to the long-term success of the project, emphasizing continuous scientific evaluation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New report on 'Project Cheetah' highlights challenges.
Issues include high mortality, habitat suitability, human-wildlife conflict.
Recommends adaptive management, monitoring, genetic diversity.
Cheetahs extinct in India since 1952.
Kuno National Park is a key reintroduction site.
mainsPoints: Critical analysis of the scientific and logistical challenges in reintroduction programs for large carnivores.
Importance of habitat assessment and prey base availability for the long-term success of such projects.
Role of community participation and mitigation of human-wildlife conflict in conservation efforts.
Lessons learned from 'Project Cheetah' for future wildlife conservation initiatives in India.
Ethical considerations and ecological impacts of species reintroduction.
GS3.imageDescription: A cheetah walking through tall grass in a savanna-like environment.
GS3.trivia: Cheetahs are the world's fastest land animals, capable of running at speeds of up to 120 km/h over short distances.

GS3.headline: India's Services Sector Growth Remains Robust in October, PMI Data Indicates
GS3.explainer:
• India's services sector continued its strong growth trajectory in October, with the S&P Global India Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) remaining robust at 60.5.
• This marks a sustained period of expansion, driven by strong demand conditions, new business gains, and improved business confidence.
